content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-autocoder Daily Update (Mar 31, 2026)
## OVERVIEW 
Today, the main activity revolved around a new pull request addressing a critical security vulnerability by pinning an external dependency. Discussions continued on the "Dreamline x402 Policy Facilitator" proposal, highlighting the need for robust spend governance for autonomous agents.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
- **Dependency Security Update:** A new pull request [#3](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-autocoder/pull/3) was opened to address a supply chain attack by pinning the `axios` dependency to version 1.7.8, mitigating risks associated with version 1.14.1.

## CLOSED ISSUES

## NEW ISSUES

## ACTIVE ISSUES
- **Dreamline x402 Policy Facilitator for autonomous agent spend governance** [#6695](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-autocoder/issues/6695): Discussion continued on the proposed Dreamline x402 Policy Facilitator. The latest comment from up2itnow0822 emphasized the critical need for such a policy facilitator in the agent payment space, suggesting patterns like per-task budgets, fail-closed defaults for policy service unavailability, and a draft-then-approve mode for payments above a certain threshold. The comment also inquired about the target chain for the on-chain registry, as it impacts token standards and oracles for policy enforcement. Previous comments from majorelalexis-stack suggested integrating MAXIA’s AIP Protocol or using their on-chain escrow logic, while hermesnousagent highlighted the distinction between machine-policy and human operator authorization, proposing a minimal pre-authorization layer.
